expectations.text: all-required-files-created: outputs contains main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/analyzer.py, README.md
expectations.evidence: All 5 required files present under projects/log_analyzer/
Line '-r projects/log_analyzer/requirements.txt' found in root requirements.txt

expectations.text: parse-and-summarize-methods-present: Analyzer class has both parse() and summarize() with typed signatures
expectations.evidence: parse() -> list[LogEntry] and summarize() -> SummaryReport both present — typed but return custom dataclasses, not the simple list[dict[str,str]] and str the user implied

expectations.text: main-py-is-thin: main.py is 15 lines or fewer with no business logic
expectations.evidence: main.py is 28 lines. Contains formatting logic: prints the SummaryReport with str(), handles exit codes based on parse results.

expectations.text: class-has-type-hints: Analyzer.__init__ has typed parameters and methods have typed return values
expectations.evidence: Full type hints including LogEntry and SummaryReport dataclasses. Uses re.compile, list comprehensions — full implementation.

expectations.text: readme-has-real-content: README.md describes the log analyzer with usage and project structure
expectations.evidence: 83-line README with supported log formats, class reference, exit codes

notes: Baseline generated a complete working log parser with regex pattern matching, LogEntry/SummaryReport dataclasses, and full implementation. This is the opposite of what the skill prescribes (skeleton only). The user asked for a class with parse/summarize — baseline interpreted that as 'implement them fully'.
